headline: MACC: 1MDB Recovery Reaches RM31.3 Billion, 74.5% of RM42B Losses
context: MACC chief Azam Baki said in 2025 that Malaysia had recovered RM31.3 billion of an estimated RM42 billion in 1MDB and SRC-linked losses. Within that, the MOF reported RM20.73 billion came from settlements with financial institutions including Goldman Sachs, AmBank and JPMorgan.

cards.big: 1MDB total recovery has reached RM31.3 billion, 74.5% of the RM42 billion MACC estimates was misappropriated.
cards.sub: MOF October 2025: RM20.73 billion of that came from settlements with Goldman Sachs, AmBank and JPMorgan; the rest from asset seizures and prosecutions.

cards.big: Goldman Sachs agreed to a USD3.9 billion settlement in 2020: USD2.5 billion cash plus a USD1.4 billion asset-recovery guarantee.
cards.sub: It is the largest single 1MDB-related financial-institution settlement to date.

cards.big: Recovery flows from settlements with financial institutions and asset seizures across multiple jurisdictions.
cards.sub: The US Department of Justice has separately recovered and returned about USD1.4 billion in 1MDB-linked assets to Malaysia.

cards.big: DOJ says more than USD4.5 billion was misappropriated. MACC's Malaysian estimate is RM42 billion in 1MDB and SRC-linked losses.
cards.sub: Recovery comparisons depend on which baseline is used. Multiple jurisdictions have ongoing litigation that may produce further recoveries.

cards.big: The recovery story is no longer 'how little came back'. It is whether the recovered funds reach public accounts and institutional reform follows.
RM31.3 billion recovered, 74.5% of estimated losses. The remaining test is whether the institutions that enabled the scandal have been structurally changed.
